## PR13: Support Artifact Stale/Degraded Status Propagation

### Scope

Adds substrate support for downstream artifact freshness tracking and staleness propagation.

When an upstream `accepted_story_ledger_v1` artifact changes `source_hash`, dependent support artifacts for the same project/job/manuscript are marked `STALE` instead of crashing or silently rendering outdated data.

### Why

Review Gate corrections can change the accepted ledger. Long-form support artifacts such as `story_shape_signal_map_v1` and `manuscript_signal_appendix_v1` must expose freshness state so downstream UI/runtime can decide whether to regenerate, render degraded, or render normally.

### In scope

- Add `ArtifactFreshnessStatus` type in a dedicated freshness module.
- Add `RuntimeArtifactEnvelopeWithFreshness` extension type.
- Add `freshness_status` column to `evaluation_artifacts` if missing.
- Add `artifact_dependencies` table.
- Seed dependencies:
  - `accepted_story_ledger_v1` → `story_shape_signal_map_v1`
  - `accepted_story_ledger_v1` → `manuscript_signal_appendix_v1`
- Add staleness propagation trigger on `source_hash` changes.
- Add `checkArtifactFreshness(...)` runtime guard.
- Add unit tests for current, stale, degraded, and legacy freshness behavior.

### Out of scope

- No active pipeline rewiring.
- No artifact writer adoption yet.
- No UI changes.
- No destructive changes to existing artifact persistence.
- No duplicate artifact table.

### Files changed

- `lib/evaluation/artifacts/artifactFreshness.ts`
- `lib/evaluation/orchestration/propagationGuard.ts`
- `supabase/migrations/20260522030000_artifact_staleness_propagation.sql`
- `__tests__/evaluation/artifactPropagation.test.ts`

### Reviewer note

The shared `RuntimeArtifactEnvelope` was not rewritten in this PR. Instead, this PR adds a non-breaking extension type so legacy artifact envelopes remain valid while freshness-aware code can opt in explicitly.
